questions.q: The Civil Disobedience Movement began with which famous 1930 event?
questions.explanation: The Civil Disobedience Movement began with the Salt March to Dandi in 1930.

questions.q: Who led the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.q: In which year did the Civil Disobedience Movement begin?
questions.explanation: The Civil Disobedience Movement began in 1930.

questions.q: A core method of the Civil Disobedience Movement was to do what?
options: Take up arms against the British
Deliberately and nonviolently break colonial laws
questions.explanation: The movement centred on the deliberate, nonviolent breaking of unjust colonial laws.

questions.q: The Civil Disobedience Movement encouraged Indians to boycott what?
questions.explanation: The movement called for boycotting British goods as part of its programme.

questions.q: Which group participated in unusually large numbers during the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.explanation: The movement mobilized people across the country, including large numbers of women.

questions.q: The first law deliberately broken to launch the Civil Disobedience Movement was the:
questions.explanation: The movement was launched by breaking the salt law at Dandi.

questions.q: From which ashram did the march that launched the Civil Disobedience Movement begin?
questions.explanation: The launching Salt March began from Sabarmati Ashram near Ahmedabad.

questions.q: What was the guiding spirit of the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.explanation: The movement was guided by nonviolence, following Gandhi's principle of ahimsa.

questions.q: Breaking 'unjust laws' nonviolently was the central idea of which movement?
questions.explanation: Nonviolently breaking unjust laws was the central idea of the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.q: On what date did Gandhi break the salt law, marking the start of the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.explanation: Gandhi broke the salt law at Dandi on 6 April 1930, launching the movement.

questions.q: The Civil Disobedience Movement followed which Congress declaration made at Lahore?
questions.explanation: The movement followed the Congress's 1929 Lahore declaration of Purna Swaraj, or complete independence.

questions.q: Which 1931 pact led to the first suspension of the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.explanation: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact of March 1931 led to the suspension of the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.q: After the Gandhi-Irwin Pact, the movement was revived in 1932 following the failure of which talks?
questions.explanation: The movement was revived in 1932 after the Second Round Table Conference failed to satisfy Congress demands.

questions.q: Besides salt, defiance during the movement included the non-payment of which colonial charge?
questions.explanation: The movement included no-tax and no-rent campaigns refusing to pay land revenue and other taxes.

questions.q: In the North-West Frontier, the Civil Disobedience Movement was strongly supported by which group?
questions.explanation: The Khudai Khidmatgars, led by Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, strongly backed the movement in the North-West Frontier.

questions.q: Which colonial monopoly besides salt was targeted by defiance, such as tapping toddy and breaking forest laws?
questions.explanation: Protesters also defied forest laws and excise restrictions as part of the broader civil disobedience programme.

questions.q: What happened to Gandhi soon after the movement gathered momentum in 1930?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was arrested and imprisoned in May 1930 as the movement spread.

questions.q: The Civil Disobedience Movement differed from the earlier Non-Cooperation Movement chiefly because it involved:
options: Actively breaking laws rather than only withdrawing cooperation
questions.explanation: Unlike Non-Cooperation, which withdrew consent, Civil Disobedience involved actively breaking specific laws.

questions.q: The boycott of foreign cloth during the movement promoted the use of which Indian product?
questions.explanation: The boycott of foreign cloth promoted the use of handspun, handwoven khadi.

questions.q: Which conference in London ran parallel to the Civil Disobedience Movement to discuss India's constitution?
questions.explanation: The Round Table Conferences in London discussed constitutional reform while the movement unfolded in India.

questions.q: When was the Civil Disobedience Movement finally withdrawn?
questions.explanation: After its revival, the Civil Disobedience Movement was formally withdrawn by 1934.

questions.q: Which Viceroy was in office when the Civil Disobedience Movement began in 1930?
questions.explanation: Lord Irwin was Viceroy when the Civil Disobedience Movement began in 1930.

questions.q: Which Viceroy oversaw the harsh suppression after the movement's 1932 revival?
questions.explanation: Lord Willingdon, who succeeded Irwin, oversaw the harsh suppression of the revived movement in 1932.

questions.q: A precondition Gandhi placed before launching the movement was an eleven-point list of demands to which official?
questions.explanation: Gandhi presented eleven demands to Viceroy Lord Irwin before launching the movement, including reduction of the salt tax.

questions.q: Which industrial city saw a notable workers' and communist-influenced involvement during the early 1930s civil disobedience phase?
questions.explanation: Sholapur in Bombay Presidency saw intense protests in 1930, with workers briefly taking control of the town.

questions.q: The Karachi session of the Congress in 1931 endorsed the Gandhi-Irwin Pact and adopted a resolution on what?
questions.explanation: The 1931 Karachi session endorsed the pact and adopted the landmark resolution on Fundamental Rights and Economic Policy.

questions.q: Which woman activist is associated with leading salt and civil-disobedience protests and later social work, having sold contraband salt in 1930?
questions.explanation: Kamaladevi Chattopadhyay openly sold contraband salt in 1930 and was a prominent organizer in the movement.

questions.q: The movement's revival in 1932 was triggered partly by the government's arrest of Gandhi soon after he returned from which event?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was arrested in January 1932 soon after returning from the Second Round Table Conference, reigniting the movement.

questions.q: Manipur and Nagaland's Rani Gaidinliu led a parallel anti-colonial movement during this period; she was associated with resistance in which region?
options: The Northeast hills (Naga and Zeliangrong areas)
questions.explanation: Rani Gaidinliu led a Zeliangrong tribal anti-colonial resistance in the Northeast hills during the civil disobedience era.
